items.q: What does specific latent heat measure?
items.correct: The energy required to change the state of 1 kg of a substance without changing its temperature.
items.misconception: The energy required to raise the temperature of 1 kg by 1 degree.
items.random_wrong: The temperature at which a substance melts or boils.
items.why: Students confuse specific latent heat with specific heat capacity because both involve 'heat' and 'kg', missing the no-temperature-change aspect of latent heat.

items.q: What happens to the total resistance when you add another resistor in parallel?
items.correct: Total resistance decreases — there are more paths for current to flow.
items.misconception: Adding any resistor always increases the total resistance.
items.random_wrong: Adding a parallel resistor doubles the voltage available.
items.why: Students apply the series rule (resistances add) to parallel circuits because they don't visualise multiple paths reducing the overall opposition.

items.q: What happens to the current as it goes around a series circuit?
items.correct: It stays the same at every point — current is conserved.
items.misconception: Current gets used up by each component as it flows around the circuit.
items.random_wrong: Current splits in half at the battery so each direction gets some.
items.why: Students confuse current (charge flow) with energy (which is transferred), thinking the bulb 'uses up' current rather than energy.
The answer examiners want is: 5.67×10⁻⁸. “9.8” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Wien's law: λ_max·T = constant means hotter objects emit:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Shorter λ. “Longer λ” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Sun's surface T ≈ 5800 K. Wien λ_max in nm (b = 2.9e-3 m K):
items.why: The real figure is 500. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
